methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
T1 Energy (formerly FREYR Battery) is a public-stage climate and energy company founded by Tom Einar Jensen that has raised over $1 billion to date. The valuation universe for comparison consists of 1 public peer (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers in adjacent technology areas, including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion.

A peer-median triangulation approach is not applicable in this case. The Climate & Energy sector contains 21 total public and private comparables, but the filtered peer set—narrowed by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier—yields fewer than 3 datapoints, falling below the minimum threshold for reliable median estimation. Specifically, only 2 same-stage peers were identified, with 0 adjacent-stage and 0 similar-tier peers meeting inclusion criteria. Expanding to an all-sector median would introduce substantial heterogeneity bias and obscure sector-specific dynamics.

Without sufficient narrow-peer data to support quantitative triangulation, valuation determination for T1 Energy requires alternative approaches beyond this dataset, such as discounted cash flow analysis, precedent transaction review, or detailed comparative analysis of the single public comparable. The key uncertainty remains the company's differentiation within a sparse public-comp universe and the appropriate application of private-peer multiples to a now-public entity.
